title: cuscuta costaricensis
intro: Cuscuta costaricensis, also known as dodder, is a parasitic plant species that obtains its nutrients by connecting to the vascular tissues of its host plant. Cuscuta costaricensis has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial effects. The health benefits of Cuscuta costaricensis include its potential to reduce blood sugar levels, lower cholesterol, and improve cardiovascular health. In herbal preparations, Cuscuta costaricensis is often used as a dried extract or a tea, and it may also be incorporated into capsules or tinctures for its medicinal and health benefits.
